thread.title: Effective challenge: Epic Sepsis Model live alerting at Northgate (sealed session 3)
thread.question: Should Northgate Health System deploy the Epic Sepsis Model for live clinical sepsis alerting across its inpatient units?

evidence.finding: Packet item E1 (see pilot-dryrun/evidence-packet.md for the full dated, sourced item).
Packet item E2 (see pilot-dryrun/evidence-packet.md for the full dated, sourced item).
Packet item E3 (see pilot-dryrun/evidence-packet.md for the full dated, sourced item).
Packet item E4 (see pilot-dryrun/evidence-packet.md for the full dated, sourced item).
Packet item E5 (see pilot-dryrun/evidence-packet.md for the full dated, sourced item).
Packet item E6 (see pilot-dryrun/evidence-packet.md for the full dated, sourced item).
Packet item E7 (see pilot-dryrun/evidence-packet.md for the full dated, sourced item).

assumption.text: A single-unit, silent-run-first, human-in-the-loop pilot bounds patient harm and is materially reversible compared to house-wide autonomous alerting; the packet does not supply Northgate-specific operational detail.

Northgate possesses sufficient local historical encounter data and informatics capacity to train and validate v2 locally as Epic recommends (E3); this capacity is assumed, not evidenced in the packet.

claim.text: v2's structural changes (local training, revised onset definition, reduced antibiotic-order reliance) target the mechanisms blamed for v1's failures, so v1's poor external numbers do not automatically transfer to a locally-trained v2.
The relevant counterfactual is unaided clinician recognition against a high preventable-death base rate, not a perfect model, so a credible early-warning layer has real upside.
A favorable single-center before/after deployment exists: threshold >=5, sensitivity 86%, specificity 80.8%, local AUC 0.834, associated with 44% lower odds of sepsis mortality.
Local training plus local threshold-setting plus a mandatory local validation gate directly answer the population-mismatch failures seen in external v1 validations.
Limiting scope to one pilot unit with silent-run-first and human-in-the-loop alerting makes the decision reversible and bounds harm during evidence generation.

objection.assumption: Proposal rests on a configuration (locally-trained v2) for which no independent external validation exists (E7); the only favorable numbers cited are v1-or-unspecified (E1) and design intent (E3), not v2 performance.
objection.text: [BLOCKING] Proposal rests on a configuration (locally-trained v2) for which no independent external validation exists (E7); the only favorable numbers cited are v1-or-unspecified (E1) and design intent (E3), not v2 performance. Consequence: Live alerting could replicate v1's external failure (E4: AUC 0.63, 67% missed) with no prior evidence it will not. Verification: Local validation study of locally-trained v2 on Northgate data reporting discrimination and calibration against pre-registered floors (GATE-1).

objection.assumption: E1 is a before/after design that cannot isolate the model's contribution from co-occurring sepsis-program changes, and the model version is unspecified.
objection.text: [NON-BLOCKING] E1 is a before/after design that cannot isolate the model's contribution from co-occurring sepsis-program changes, and the model version is unspecified. Consequence: The 44% mortality-odds reduction cannot be attributed to the model, let alone to v2. Verification: A controlled or version-specified study; absent that, E1 is downgraded to hypothesis-generating per its own authors.

objection.assumption: Locally-trained v2 requires a local label definition and feature pipeline that does not yet exist; the original model's antibiotic-order-timing leakage (E6) can recur, and vendor opacity limits inspectability.
objection.text: [BLOCKING] Locally-trained v2 requires a local label definition and feature pipeline that does not yet exist; the original model's antibiotic-order-timing leakage (E6) can recur, and vendor opacity limits inspectability. Consequence: A locally-trained model could again 'predict' sepsis only after clinicians act, yielding late or circular alerts with no clinical lead time. Verification: Documented local lineage, label/feature definitions, and a leakage audit confirming no dependence on post-treatment signals (GATE-2).

objection.assumption: E1 explicitly does not state model version, so citing it for a v2 deployment is a lineage error the data-skeptic must catch.
objection.text: [NON-BLOCKING] E1 explicitly does not state model version, so citing it for a v2 deployment is a lineage error the data-skeptic must catch. Consequence: Mistaking v1/unspecified evidence for v2 evidence inflates the apparent support for the proposal. Verification: Version attribution in the source; none available.

objection.assumption: False-negative harm: external validations missed 67% (E4) and ~50% pre-onset (E5) of sepsis patients; a deployed alert can induce false reassurance and displace clinical vigilance.
objection.text: [BLOCKING] False-negative harm: external validations missed 67% (E4) and ~50% pre-onset (E5) of sepsis patients; a deployed alert can induce false reassurance and displace clinical vigilance. Consequence: Patients whom the model misses may receive less attention than under unaided recognition — net harm rather than benefit. Verification: Local sensitivity at the chosen threshold plus a protocol keeping alerting as adjunct to, not replacement for, clinical screening (GATE-1, GATE-3).

objection.assumption: Equity: v1 performed worst (sensitivity 14.7%) in a 59% Hispanic / 26% Black, lower-SES population (E5), indicating population mismatch and disparate miss rates.
objection.text: [NON-BLOCKING] Equity: v1 performed worst (sensitivity 14.7%) in a 59% Hispanic / 26% Black, lower-SES population (E5), indicating population mismatch and disparate miss rates. Consequence: Deployment could systematically under-detect sepsis in marginalized subpopulations. Verification: Subgroup performance and harm analysis on local data across race/ethnicity/SES (GATE-3).

objection.assumption: False-positive burden: external alert rate was 18% of hospitalizations with PPV 12% (E4), implying frequent false alarms that drive workups and patient anxiety.
objection.text: [NON-BLOCKING] False-positive burden: external alert rate was 18% of hospitalizations with PPV 12% (E4), implying frequent false alarms that drive workups and patient anxiety. Consequence: Alert fatigue and unnecessary interventions on falsely-flagged patients. Verification: Local PPV and alert-volume measurement during silent run before go-live.

objection.assumption: No monitoring, recalibration, drift-detection, incident-response, or decommissioning plan is present in the evidence; 'reversible pilot' is asserted, not engineered.
objection.text: [BLOCKING] No monitoring, recalibration, drift-detection, incident-response, or decommissioning plan is present in the evidence; 'reversible pilot' is asserted, not engineered. Consequence: Silent performance decay or drift could go undetected and harm patients indefinitely. Verification: A monitoring/recalibration/incident-response/decommissioning plan with a live drift dashboard and triggers (GATE-4).

objection.assumption: Silent-run-first and human-in-the-loop alerting are claimed but not grounded in any evidence item; they are operational assumptions.
objection.text: [NON-BLOCKING] Silent-run-first and human-in-the-loop alerting are claimed but not grounded in any evidence item; they are operational assumptions. Consequence: Without making them binding preconditions, the proposal could slide into live autonomous alerting. Verification: Written go-live protocol naming silent run and human-in-the-loop as gates.

objection.assumption: The base-rate cost of no early-warning (E2) can be weaponized to wave through an unvalidated model; CDC figures provide no time-to-treatment dose-response and so do not quantify a model's benefit.
objection.text: [NON-BLOCKING] The base-rate cost of no early-warning (E2) can be weaponized to wave through an unvalidated model; CDC figures provide no time-to-treatment dose-response and so do not quantify a model's benefit. Consequence: Approving on base-rate urgency alone would smuggle E4/E5-grade failure into production under a humanitarian banner. Verification: Whether the recommendation conditions deployment on local evidence rather than on the base rate alone.

objection.assumption: A 'reversible' pilot can ossify into permanent unvalidated dependence; clinicians come to rely on the alert and no one ever pulls it.
objection.text: [NON-BLOCKING] A 'reversible' pilot can ossify into permanent unvalidated dependence; clinicians come to rely on the alert and no one ever pulls it. Consequence: De facto house-wide reliance without the evidence that the gates were meant to require. Verification: A hard sunset date forcing an explicit deploy-or-decommission decision.

payload.resolution: Advocate conceded E1 cannot establish causal mortality benefit and cannot be cited as v2 evidence; it remains only as a reference operating point, resolving the overclaim.
Advocate accepted the lineage discipline and formally withdrew E1 as support for v2, scoping it to a non-version reference only; objection resolved by concession of scope.
Terminal disposition CONVERTED_TO_RESIDUAL_RISK: converted from an open challenge into a tracked residual risk / agreed gate recorded in the recommendation conditions; not carried as formal dissent.
Advocate added silent-run alert-burden measurement and a clinician override workflow as explicit preconditions, converting the concern into an accepted design amendment.
Advocate elevated silent-run-first and human-in-the-loop from description to binding precondition in the proposal, resolving the gap.

Defended by sequencing: the gates ensure E2's upside is pursued only after local validation, so the base rate motivates the work without authorizing an unvalidated go-live; the objection is satisfied by the gate structure itself.
Terminal disposition CONVERTED_TO_RESIDUAL_RISK: converted from an open challenge into a tracked residual risk / agreed gate recorded in the recommendation conditions; not carried as formal dissent.

decisionRequest.proposal: ESM v2, locally trained on Northgate's own data; scope limited to one pilot ICU plus its step-down (not all-inpatient, not v1); threshold set locally via decision-curve analysis after local validation rather than Epic's default >=6; safeguards: silent/shadow run first, human-in-the-loop decision support (not autonomous), mandatory local validation gate before live alerting, subgroup/equity bias audit, monitoring dashboard with drift triggers, and pre-specified decommissioning criteria.

conditions: No live alert fires before a local validation study of locally-trained v2 meets pre-registered discrimination AND calibration floors.
review.comment: Lane A endorses the recommendation only under its stated condition.

conditions: A documented local label/feature lineage with a passed leakage audit exists before the validation study is run.
review.comment: Lane B endorses the recommendation only under its stated condition.

conditions: Subgroup harm analysis is completed and the threshold is justified by an explicit false-negative/false-positive harm-asymmetry argument, with alerting kept adjunct to clinician judgment.
review.comment: Lane H endorses the recommendation only under its stated condition.

conditions: A monitoring, recalibration, incident-response, and decommissioning plan with a live drift dashboard and a hard pilot sunset is in place before go-live.
review.comment: Lane D endorses the recommendation only under its stated condition.

conditions: The gates are absolute pre-live blockers and the pilot has an immovable sunset forcing a deploy-or-decommission decision.
review.comment: Lane E endorses the recommendation only under its stated condition.

decisionRecord.summary: Approve a gated, silent-run-first pilot of locally-trained ESM v2 on a single ICU/step-down unit, with live alerting blocked until local validation, lineage/leakage, subgroup-harm, and monitoring gates clear.
decisionRecord.rationale: It advanced because the base-rate cost of no early-warning is real (E2) and v2's design changes plausibly address v1's documented failures (E3, E6), but every favorable number in the packet is either wrong-version, confounded before/after, or design-intent-only (E1, E3) while every hard external number is adverse (E4 AUC 0.63/67% missed; E5 sensitivity 14.7%) and v2 has zero independent validation (E7). Chose a gated pilot over outright deployment because deploying an unvalidated configuration at Epic's default threshold would replicate the documented harms; chose a gated pilot over do_not_deploy because the evidence against deployment is overwhelmingly against v1, not v2, and rejecting on v1 evidence would forfeit E2's base-rate benefit on a version mismatch. Chose locally-set decision-curve thresholds over Epic's default >=6 because that default missed 67% of cases (E4).
conditions: GATE-1: Local validation study of locally-trained v2 on Northgate data reporting AUC, sensitivity, specificity, PPV/NPV and a calibration curve must meet pre-registered floors before any live alert fires.

GATE-2: Documented local data lineage, feature and label definitions, plus a passed leakage audit confirming no dependence on post-treatment signals (e.g., antibiotic-order timing) before the validation study is run.
GATE-3: Subgroup performance and harm-asymmetry analysis across race/ethnicity/SES strata, with explicit false-negative vs false-positive threshold justification.
GATE-4: Monitoring, recalibration, incident-response and decommissioning plan with a live drift dashboard and a hard pilot sunset date, in place before go-live and reviewed quarterly.
Alerting remains adjunct decision support to clinician judgment; it must not displace existing clinical sepsis vigilance.
No house-wide live alerting and no v1 deployment are approved under this recommendation.
decisionRecord.nextAction: Approve a gated, silent-run-first pilot of locally-trained ESM v2 on a single ICU/step-down unit, with live alerting blocked until local validation, lineage/leakage, subgroup-harm, and monitoring gates clear.

minorityReport.text: Minority position: the gates are necessary but the honest baseline is that this packet contains ZERO evidence that the proposed configuration (locally-trained v2) works anywhere — E7 is a true evidential void, E1 is wrong-version and confounded, E3 is design intent only, and the only hard numbers (E4, E5) are failures. A 'deploy_with_conditions' verdict risks being read as approval-in-waiting. If GATE-1 through GATE-4 are not enforced as absolute pre-live blockers with a hard sunset, the correct verdict is do_not_deploy. The residual that genuinely survives (E2x) is institutional ossification: pilots that never end. Endorsement is conditional on the gates being real and the sunset being immovable.
